features.evidence: what s up everybody i hope you are as excited as i am today cuz we are reviewing the first laptop of 2024 and the guest of honor here today is the acer swift go it s been loaded with the most current gen specification including intel s brand new core ultra 7 series processor a speedy 16 gb of lpddr 5x memory we also have a reasonably generous 1 tb ssd intel s integrated arc graphics of course we ve also got wi fi 6e and bluetooth five standards and this is a 14 in display as you can tell this laptop has a lot of new stuff packed into it but we re going to answer the ultimate question of what you can expect with the acer swifts go it is technically a premium tier laptop so we re going to treat it as such and give you guys a honest rundown we always do starting with a quick tour of the packaging experience things are looking as generic as ever you get a plain old cardboard box in typical acer fashion but it s inside what counts right because you will find behind a ton of protective packaging the acer swift s go itself it s definitely a petite looking laptop but more than that in a minute you also have a standard 65 wat charging adapter though this year you get usbc charging out of the box which is nice to see you also have the standard w outlet charging cable piece and then of course a quick start guide with some basic warranty and regulation information now look i really don t want to hate on the design here i don t really have a problem with the general looks of this machine the problem is that acer has been recycling the same design elements from previous swift and aspire models for nearly a decade and this laptop just feels old news it s not refreshing in terms of its looks it s not a new design most other brands have at least put some effort in renewing their design acer just doesn t seem to care much in this factor now with that said you do have a full metallic exterior giving it a more premium feel and finish the laptop has a overall weight of 132 kg which is about as heavy as you expect your average 14 inch laptop to be and should be good for all day carrying again starting with the front side or the top side i should say you ll notice you have a clean flat surface no particular texture that iconic silver color was with most acer laptops and the branding on the top side iort diversity is fantastic on this laptop so on one side you have two fully loaded thunderbolt 4 usbc ports you also have a hdmi 2 However, you the battery life is crazier and i know there are luna lake laptops at higher prices that bring this battery life but this express variant even beat something the core ultra 5 226p in both single core and multi core gigb test which is crazy

1 port and of course a usba super speed port with reverse charging on the other side you have a kensington lock you have one more usba super speed port you have the good old headphone jack and a micro sd card reader the bottom side of this laptop has a fairly thin enclosure you ll notice you have two long air intake vents in a linear fashion because you do have a dual fan setup so plenty of air flow we ll talk about that a little bit later on you ll also notice to have two speakers one or either corner this is a bottom firing speaker setup in a stereo configuration and rest assured we will do a sound test closer to the end of the video unfolding this laptop presents you with a pretty nice looking inner chassis so you have that clean metallic exterior combined with that silver color to give a pretty gracious look the problem is it s bered with stickers all over which totally kill the vibe now at the center you have a larger than life track pattern at first glance this looks a glass sur trackpad however your eyes are fooling you because it is very much a plastic surface one on top of that there is some degree of flex towards the corners unfortunately the e clicks are tactile but this trackpad just fall short of something you d expect on a premium tiar laptop this acer has definitely improved their keyboard game a little bit with this laptop so the first thing you ll notice is that the keyc cap seem to be slightly better labeled the font a little bit more bold a little bit brighter also you have a decent amount of surface area per key cap additionally the keyboard is of course is fully backed in multiple brightness settings now since it is a 14in laptop you won t get a 10 keypad but that s standard practice and you have a built in fingerprint scanner as well what i do about this keyboard a lot is that acer has elevated the overall height of each key cap which gives you a deeper key travel and a more tactile keystroke thereby providing a pretty decent typing experience it s not going to be as good as he likolo noo idea pad series or macbook of course but it is still nonetheless a decent attempt now we come to one huge design flaw and that s the fact that acer continues to put the heat exhaust vents directly beneath the lcd screen in the past this has had repercussions in the long term wear and tear and i guarantee you for some people it will still be a problem which is a bit of an unfortunate thing on acter side now as far as the hinge quality goes it s definitely nice it s sturdy there s not a lot of wobble it s well tuned as long as you re generally gentle with it it should last you for the years to come and then of course we come to the display fitting itself it s reasonable enough in my opinion so you have a fairly thin chin at the bottom with some basic branding you ll also appreciate the fact that the bezels are more or less in line with modern day standard so they re nice and thin in terms of overall thickness and then again a fairly thin forehead at the top what i love here is that quad h resolution webcam it is really crisp it s clean and you can tell it s a high quality camera because even with medium to low lighting there is very little noise acer is really leading the charge with this laptop over here and i hope all other brands and manufacturers really pick up this practice let s talk about the display itself now key distinction this is not the oled model that s an entirely separate model of the acer swift go this here is the standard ips lcd screen with a full hd plus resolution you also have a 16 x10 aspect ratio as a result and it s worth noting you do have a base refresh rate of 60 hz unfortunately with this so you only get the higher refresh rate with the oled panel additionally you have a peak brightness of 300 nits which means this laptop isn t super bright but it s bright enough overall additionally you ll find that the viewing angles are also quite nice and the blacks are also pretty well done and then i do want to also note that this is a touch captive display so yes you can use the touchcreen and it s fairly responsive all things considered and it keeps a matte finish at that now the overall color rating is also pretty good you have a 100 srgb rating it s not 100 adobe rgb unfortunately so if you are using it for extreme creative purposes you will find the colors a little bit of l in but for general viewing the colors are sharp and vivid enough a quick performance recap so we ve got intel s brand new meteor le core ultra7 processor based on a new architecture we also of course have 16 gb of lp ddr5x memory and then past that we do have intel s integrated ark igpu now all this translates to the usual day to day performance you re not going to notice any improvement obviously everything feels fast and snappy whether it s browsing the web or doing some word productivity however you will find that other niche tasks have subtle improvements program or coding for example which feels a little bit more responsive and more capable and then there s big improvements in areas doing 3d animation where thanks to the accelerated performance of the igpu you get a lot better performance in general now one area where i wasn t as impressed is the king of all activities on a laptop multi layer 10 bit 4k video editing on daini resolve i was expecting this to be a substantial improvement but i still find the performance is more or less similar to what you would get on a 13th generation i7 u series chip you have a little bit of lag if you have too many layers of 10bit footage and it s not particularly faster though it is worth noting that render times have considerably improved and that itself is a credible improvement it s also worth noting of course this is a semi new architecture so there will be subtle improvements over time as companies optimize their programs for this brand new chip now one area where i was really impressed is gaming that arc integrated graphics set is really killing it here the fact that you can play games fortnite at medium to high settings and almost hit 60 fps the whole way through is something that was unheard of a couple years back this is definitely an area where intel has been innovating it s nice to see the igpu game comes so far from what it used to be another area where you see substantial improvement is thermals so intel chips are notoriously known for being the hottest of the bunch no more is that the case we found under unrealistic peak loads we only hit a maximum average surface temperature of 40 c and under more realistic loads this laptop stays around the 35 c mark these are much cooler numbers than 13th generation hardware fan noise unfortunately is going to be fairly high at around 50 db under peak load and that frequently happens this is of course because you have a dual fan setup but i d rather have louder fan noise than a hotter laptop generally speaking upgradeability is fairly limited so you cannot upgrade the ram that s entirely soldered on board however you can upgrade the existing nvme ssd you have that same slot so you can change that around and it s also know you can technically also upgrade the wi fi module acer provides you with a 65w hour battery here and again colossal improvements based on our real world test we got up to 12 and 1 half hours of battery life doing day to day standard activities which again is about 4 hours more on average than previous generation hardware now the speaker quality unfortunately isn t all that great it sounds a pretty generic standard stereo speaker set up there s not a lot of depth in the overall quality i ve done a quick sound test for your reference give me a sign will everything be all right i need a sign cuz i don t want to waste my time sometimes i don t want to wake up i feel tired in my bed want to lay up watch tv instead about fake love let with my bra retailing at approximately 1,000 this is definitely premium range category and the swift go is a laptop that achieves some things but also cuts a lot of corners for example the design here feels dated it has some major flaws having a heat exhaust vent facing upwards and just isn t the brightest looking laptop of the badge however on the other side fantastic port diversification combined with a respectable display a good keyboard and of course fantastic battery life alongside with generational improvements in the performance sector really make this a great laptop on the other hand it s really going to come down to what you want if your key priority is maximum performance then this is a laptop that i think has consistent improvements but if you re trying to look for a laptop that feels new feels more refreshed this is not the best one out there it s really feeling dated i said so it s just going to depend on your personal priorities let me know what you think of the acer swift go14 and i will leave purchase links below as well as always guys if you enjoyed the content please consider subscribing to this channel and liking this video it genuinely helps me grow

what s up everybody i hope you are as excited as i am today cuz we are reviewing the first laptop of 2024 and the guest of honor here today is the acer swift go it s been loaded with the most current gen specification including intel s brand new core ultra 7 series processor a speedy 16 gb of lpddr 5x memory we also have a reasonably generous 1 tb ssd intel s integrated arc graphics of course we ve also got wi fi 6e and bluetooth five standards and this is a 14 in display as you can tell this laptop has a lot of new stuff packed into it but we re going to answer the ultimate question of what you can expect with the acer swifts go it is technically a premium tier laptop so we re going to treat it as such and give you guys a honest rundown we always do starting with a quick tour of the packaging experience things are looking as generic as ever you get a plain old cardboard box in typical acer fashion but it s inside what counts right because you will find behind a ton of protective packaging the acer swift s go itself it s definitely a petite looking laptop but more than that in a minute you also have a standard 65 wat charging adapter though this year you get usbc charging out of the box which is nice to see you also have the standard w outlet charging cable piece and then of course a quick start guide with some basic warranty and regulation information now look i really don t want to hate on the design here i don t really have a problem with the general looks of this machine the problem is that acer has been recycling the same design elements from previous swift and aspire models for nearly a decade and this laptop just feels old news it s not refreshing in terms of its looks it s not a new design most other brands have at least put some effort in renewing their design acer just doesn t seem to care much in this factor now with that said you do have a full metallic exterior giving it a more premium feel and finish the laptop has a overall weight of 132 kg which is about as heavy as you expect your average 14 inch laptop to be and should be good for all day carrying again starting with the front side or the top side i should say you ll notice you have a clean flat surface no particular texture that iconic silver color was with most acer laptops and the branding on the top side iort diversity is fantastic on this laptop so on one side you have two fully loaded thunderbolt 4 usbc ports you also have a hdmi 2

course is fully backed in multiple brightness settings now since it is a 14in laptop you won t get a 10 keypad but that s standard practice and you have a built in fingerprint scanner as well what i do about this keyboard a lot is that acer has elevated the overall height of each key cap which gives you a deeper key travel and a more tactile keystroke thereby providing a pretty decent typing experience it s not going to be as good as he likolo noo idea pad series or macbook of course but it is still nonetheless a decent attempt now we come to one huge design flaw and that s the fact that acer continues to put the heat exhaust vents directly beneath the lcd screen in the past this has had repercussions in the long term wear and tear and i guarantee you for some people it will still be a problem which is a bit of an unfortunate thing on acter side now as far as the hinge quality goes it s definitely nice it s sturdy there s not a lot of wobble it s well tuned as long as you re generally gentle with it it should last you for the years to come and then of course we come to the display fitting itself it s reasonable enough in my opinion so you have a fairly thin chin at the bottom with some basic branding you ll also appreciate the fact that the bezels are more or less in line with modern day standard so they re nice and thin in terms of overall thickness and then again a fairly thin forehead at the top what i love here is that quad h resolution webcam it is really crisp it s clean and you can tell it s a high quality camera because even with medium to low lighting there is very little noise acer is really leading the charge with this laptop over here and i hope all other brands and manufacturers really pick up this practice let s talk about the display itself now key distinction this is not the oled model that s an entirely separate model of the acer swift go this here is the standard ips lcd screen with a full hd plus resolution you also have a 16 x10 aspect ratio as a result and it s worth noting you do have a base refresh rate of 60 hz unfortunately with this so you only get the higher refresh rate with the oled panel additionally you have a peak brightness of 300 nits which means this laptop isn t super bright but it s bright enough overall additionally you ll find that the viewing angles are also quite nice and the blacks are also pretty well done and then i do want to also note that this is a touch captive display so yes you can use the touchcreen and it s fairly responsive all things considered and it keeps a matte finish at that now the overall color rating is also pretty good you have a 100 srgb rating it s not 100 adobe rgb unfortunately so if you are using it for extreme creative purposes you will find the colors a little bit of l in but for general viewing the colors are sharp and vivid enough a quick performance recap so we ve got intel s brand new meteor le core ultra7 processor based on a new architecture we also of course have 16 gb of lp ddr5x memory and then past that we do have intel s integrated ark igpu now all this translates to the usual day to day performance you re not going to notice any improvement obviously everything feels fast and snappy whether it s browsing the web or doing some word productivity however you will find that other niche tasks have subtle improvements program or coding for example which feels a little bit more responsive and more capable and then there s big improvements in areas doing 3d animation where thanks to the accelerated performance of the igpu you get a lot better performance in general now one area where i wasn t as impressed is the king of all activities on a laptop multi layer 10 bit 4k video editing on daini resolve i was expecting this to be a substantial improvement but i still find the performance is more or less similar to what you would get on a 13th generation i7 u series chip you have a little bit of lag if you have too many layers of 10bit footage and it s not particularly faster though it is worth noting that render times have considerably improved and that itself is a credible improvement it s also worth noting of course this is a semi new architecture so there will be subtle improvements over time as companies optimize their programs for this brand new chip now one area where i was really impressed is gaming that arc integrated graphics set is really killing it here the fact that you can play games fortnite at medium to high settings and almost hit 60 fps the whole way through is something that was unheard of a couple years back this is definitely an area where intel has been innovating it s nice to see the igpu game comes so far from what it used to be another area where you see substantial improvement is thermals so intel chips are notoriously known for being the hottest of the bunch no more is that the case we found under unrealistic peak loads we only hit a maximum average surface temperature of 40 c and under more realistic loads this laptop stays around the 35 c mark these are much cooler numbers than 13th generation hardware fan noise unfortunately is going to be fairly high at around 50 db under peak load and that frequently happens this is of course because you have a dual fan setup but i d rather have louder fan noise than a hotter laptop generally speaking upgradeability is fairly limited so you cannot upgrade the ram that s entirely soldered on board however you can upgrade the existing nvme ssd you have that same slot so you can change that around and it s also know you can technically also upgrade the wi fi module acer provides you with a 65w hour battery here and again colossal improvements based on our real world test we got up to 12 and 1 half hours of battery life doing day to day standard activities which again is about 4 hours more on average than previous generation hardware now the speaker quality unfortunately isn t all that great it sounds a pretty generic standard stereo speaker set up there s not a lot of depth in the overall quality i ve done a quick sound test for your reference give me a sign will everything be all right i need a sign cuz i don t want to waste my time sometimes i don t want to wake up i feel tired in my bed want to lay up watch tv instead about fake love let with my bra retailing at approximately 1,000 this is definitely premium range category and the swift go is a laptop that achieves some things but also cuts a lot of corners for example the design here feels dated it has some major flaws having a heat exhaust vent facing upwards and just isn t the brightest looking laptop of the badge however on the other side fantastic port diversification combined with a respectable display a good keyboard and of course fantastic battery life alongside with generational improvements in the performance sector really make this a great laptop on the other hand it s really going to come down to what you want if your key priority is maximum performance then this is a laptop that i think has consistent improvements but if you re trying to look for a laptop that feels new feels more refreshed this is not the best one out there it s really feeling dated i said so it s just going to depend on your personal priorities let me know what you think of the acer swift go14 and i will leave purchase links below as well as always guys if you enjoyed the content please consider subscribing to this channel and liking this video it genuinely helps me grow

bit of l in but for general viewing the colors are sharp and vivid enough a quick performance recap so we ve got intel s brand new meteor le core ultra7 processor based on a new architecture we also of course have 16 gb of lp ddr5x memory and then past that we do have intel s integrated ark igpu now all this translates to the usual day to day performance you re not going to notice any improvement obviously everything feels fast and snappy whether it s browsing the web or doing some word productivity however you will find that other niche tasks have subtle improvements program or coding for example which feels a little bit more responsive and more capable and then there s big improvements in areas doing 3d animation where thanks to the accelerated performance of the igpu you get a lot better performance in general now one area where i wasn t as impressed is the king of all activities on a laptop multi layer 10 bit 4k video editing on daini resolve i was expecting this to be a substantial improvement but i still find the performance is more or less similar to what you would get on a 13th generation i7 u series chip you have a little bit of lag if you have too many layers of 10bit footage and it s not particularly faster though it is worth noting that render times have considerably improved and that itself is a credible improvement it s also worth noting of course this is a semi new architecture so there will be subtle improvements over time as companies optimize their programs for this brand new chip now one area where i was really impressed is gaming that arc integrated graphics set is really killing it here the fact that you can play games fortnite at medium to high settings and almost hit 60 fps the whole way through is something that was unheard of a couple years back this is definitely an area where intel has been innovating it s nice to see the igpu game comes so far from what it used to be another area where you see substantial improvement is thermals so intel chips are notoriously known for being the hottest of the bunch no more is that the case we found under unrealistic peak loads we only hit a maximum average surface temperature of 40 c and under more realistic loads this laptop stays around the 35 c mark these are much cooler numbers than 13th generation hardware fan noise unfortunately is going to be fairly high at around 50 db under peak load and that frequently happens this is of course because you have a dual fan setup but i d rather have louder fan noise than a hotter laptop generally speaking upgradeability is fairly limited so you cannot upgrade the ram that s entirely soldered on board however you can upgrade the existing nvme ssd you have that same slot so you can change that around and it s also know you can technically also upgrade the wi fi module acer provides you with a 65w hour battery here and again colossal improvements based on our real world test we got up to 12 and 1 half hours of battery life doing day to day standard activities which again is about 4 hours more on average than previous generation hardware now the speaker quality unfortunately isn t all that great it sounds a pretty generic standard stereo speaker set up there s not a lot of depth in the overall quality i ve done a quick sound test for your reference give me a sign will everything be all right i need a sign cuz i don t want to waste my time sometimes i don t want to wake up i feel tired in my bed want to lay up watch tv instead about fake love let with my bra retailing at approximately 1,000 this is definitely premium range category and the swift go is a laptop that achieves some things but also cuts a lot of corners for example the design here feels dated it has some major flaws having a heat exhaust vent facing upwards and just isn t the brightest looking laptop of the badge however on the other side fantastic port diversification combined with a respectable display a good keyboard and of course fantastic battery life alongside with generational improvements in the performance sector really make this a great laptop on the other hand it s really going to come down to what you want if your key priority is maximum performance then this is a laptop that i think has consistent improvements but if you re trying to look for a laptop that feels new feels more refreshed this is not the best one out there it s really feeling dated i said so it s just going to depend on your personal priorities let me know what you think of the acer swift go14 and i will leave purchase links below as well as always guys if you enjoyed the content please consider subscribing to this channel and liking this video it genuinely helps me grow

review_summary: what s up everybody i hope you are as excited as i am today cuz we are reviewing the first laptop of 2024 and the guest of honor here today is the acer swift go it s been loaded with the most current gen specification including intel s brand new core ultra 7 series processor a speedy 16 gb of lpddr 5x memory we also have a reasonably generous 1 tb ssd intel s integrated arc graphics of course we ve also got wi fi 6e and bluetooth five standards and this is a 14 in display as you can tell this laptop has a lot of new stuff packed into it but we re going to answer the ultimate question of what you can expect with the acer swifts go it is technically a premium tier laptop so we re going to treat it as such and give you guys a honest rundown we always do starting with a quick tour of the packaging experience things are looking as generic as ever you get a plain old cardboard box in typical acer fashion but it s inside what counts right because you will find behind a ton of protective packaging the acer swift s go itself it s definitely a petite looking laptop but more than that in a minute you also have a standard 65 wat charging adapter though this year you get usbc charging out of the box which is nice to see you also have the standard w outlet charging cable piece and then of course a quick start guide with some basic warranty and regulation information now look i really don t want to hate on the design here i don t really have a problem with the general looks of this machine the problem is that acer has been recycling the same design elements from previous swift and aspire models for nearly a decade and this laptop just feels old news it s not refreshing in terms of its looks it s not a new design most other brands have at least put some effort in renewing their design acer just doesn t seem to care much in this factor now with that said you do have a full metallic exterior giving it a more premium feel and finish the laptop has a overall weight of 132 kg which is about as heavy as you expect your average 14 inch laptop to be and should be good for all day carrying again starting with the front side or the top side i should say you ll notice you have a clean flat surface no particular texture that iconic silver color was with most acer laptops and the branding on the top side iort diversity is fantastic on this laptop so on one side you have two fully loaded thunderbolt 4 usbc ports you also have a hdmi 2 However, you the battery life is crazier and i know there are luna lake laptops at higher prices that bring this battery life but this express variant even beat something the core ultra 5 226p in both single core and multi core gigb test which is crazy 1 port and of course a usba super speed port with reverse charging on the other side you have a kensington lock you have one more usba super speed port you have the good old headphone jack and a micro sd card reader the bottom side of this laptop has a fairly thin enclosure you ll notice you have two long air intake vents in a linear fashion because you do have a dual fan setup so plenty of air flow we ll talk about that a little bit later on you ll also notice to have two speakers one or either corner this is a bottom firing speaker setup in a stereo configuration and rest assured we will do a sound test closer to the end of the video unfolding this laptop presents you with a pretty nice looking inner chassis so you have that clean metallic exterior combined with that silver color to give a pretty gracious look the problem is it s bered with stickers all over which totally kill the vibe now at the center you have a larger than life track pattern at first glance this looks a glass sur trackpad however your eyes are fooling you because it is very much a plastic surface one on top of that there is some degree of flex towards the corners unfortunately the e clicks are tactile but this trackpad just fall short of something you d expect on a premium tiar laptop this acer has definitely improved their keyboard game a little bit with this laptop so the first thing you ll notice is that the keyc cap seem to be slightly better labeled the font a little bit more bold a little bit brighter also you have a decent amount of surface area per key cap additionally the keyboard is of course is fully backed in multiple brightness settings now since it is a 14in laptop you won t get a 10 keypad but that s standard practice and you have a built in fingerprint scanner as well what i do about this keyboard a lot is that acer has elevated the overall height of each key cap which gives you a deeper key travel and a more tactile keystroke thereby providing a pretty decent typing experience it s not going to be as good as he likolo noo idea pad series or macbook of course but it is still nonetheless a decent attempt now we come to one huge design flaw and that s the fact that acer continues to put the heat exhaust vents directly beneath the lcd screen in the past this has had repercussions in the long term wear and tear and i guarantee you for some people it will still be a problem which is a bit of an unfortunate thing on acter side now as far as the hinge quality goes it s definitely nice it s sturdy there s not a lot of wobble it s well tuned as long as you re generally gentle with it it should last you for the years to come and then of course we come to the display fitting itself it s reasonable enough in my opinion so you have a fairly thin chin at the bottom with some basic branding you ll also appreciate the fact that the bezels are more or less in line with modern day standard so they re nice and thin in terms of overall thickness and then again a fairly thin forehead at the top what i love here is that quad h resolution webcam it is really crisp it s clean and you can tell it s a high quality camera because even with medium to low lighting there is very little noise acer is really leading the charge with this laptop over here and i hope all other brands and manufacturers really pick up this practice let s talk about the display itself now key distinction this is not the oled model that s an entirely separate model of the acer swift go this here is the standard ips lcd screen with a full hd plus resolution you also have a 16 x10 aspect ratio as a result and it s worth noting you do have a base refresh rate of 60 hz unfortunately with this so you only get the higher refresh rate with the oled panel additionally you have a peak brightness of 300 nits which means this
